app.title: Salary Formula
app.subtitle: Transparent, formula-driven compensation based on Management 3.0

home.headline: Transparent Salary Formula
home.intro: Make compensation transparent and formula-driven. Define factors (seniority, skills, location, performance), set weights, and calculate salaries consistently — removing the opacity and bias from salary negotiations.
home.whyText: When people don't know how salaries are determined, they assume the worst — politics, favoritism, gender gaps. A transparent formula doesn't just fix the number; it fixes the conversation. It shifts focus from negotiation power to merit factors that the organization actually values.
antipatternsList: Too many factors — keep it to 3–6 for clarity
Factors that can't be measured objectively — define clear scales
Ignoring market rates — the formula output should be benchmarked
Set-and-forget — revisit factors annually as the org evolves

builder.title: Formula Builder
builder.explainerText: Additive factors contribute proportionally to their max value (weighted). Multiplier factors are applied last as direct multipliers on the combined result.

calculator.title: Salary Calculator
calculator.formula: Formula: Base × (1 + weighted additive score) × multipliers
calculator.adjust: Adjust factor values to calculate salary
calculator.noFormula: Set up your formula first in the Formula Builder.

comparison.title: Comparison View
comparison.name: Name
comparison.noPersons: Add people above to compare salaries.
